target: Unary transcendentals Erf/Erfc/Cbrt, which called the serial eval_unary_elementwise while the hyperbolic/inverse/gamma family (sinh/cosh/tanh/asinh/acosh/atanh/lgamma/digamma/erf_inv) already used eval_unary_elementwise_parallel.
lever: Route Erf, Erfc, Cbrt through eval_unary_elementwise_parallel (the proven, op-agnostic threaded transcendental path). erf_approx is the project's own polynomial approximation and cbrt is moderately expensive — both compute-bound, so threading over elements scales. One-line dispatch changes; the helper falls back to serial below 1<<18.
isomorphism: BIT-EXACT by construction (op-agnostic helper, already covered by lgamma_parallel_bit_identical). Added erf_cbrt_parallel_bit_identical (300k dense F64: parallel Erf/Cbrt == serial eval_unary_elementwise, bit-for-bit). 1103 fj-lax tests pass.

Expm1.reason: f64::exp_m1 is a cheap libm intrinsic, memory-bound

Log1p.reason: f64::ln_1p same class as exp_m1; excluded with it

measured_and_excluded.note: Matches the prior transcendental commit's exclusion of exp/log/sin/cos (~1.9x, memory-bound, over-spawn risk). Kept serial.

decision: SHIP. Erf 7.9x, Erfc ~7.9x (same erf_approx), Cbrt 3.9x — all compute-bound transcendentals. Expm1/Log1p measured-and-excluded (<2x, cheap intrinsics). Clears Score>=2.0.
follow_ups: Multi-dim broadcast of the expensive binary ops (broadcast_binary_tensors) still serial. erf_approx itself is a crude approximation (~1e-4 vs JAX) per project memory — accuracy (not perf) is a separate open item.
